'''Martin Masella''' (born 4 November 1969) is a Tongan former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He was a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international and played at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Masella played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] as a {{rlp|pr}} in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]].  Masella captained Tonga in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="The Teams: Tonga">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/tonga.stm
|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20001110074700/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/tonga.stm
|url-status=dead
|archive-date=2000-11-10
|title=The Teams: Tonga|publisher=BBC|date=2000-01-01|access-date=2008-03-26}}</ref>

==Background==
Masella was born to factory workers.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Jackson |first1=Glenn |title=A league of their own: islanders provide NRL family with ample opportunity to cross the cultural divide |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/a-league-of-their-own-islanders-provide-nrl-family-with-ample-opportunity-to-cross-the-cultural-divide-20100624-z3lq.html |website=The Sydney Morning Herald |date=24 June 2010 |access-date=7 April 2019}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Masella played in Australia for [[Balmain Tigers]],<ref>{{cite web |last1=Kent |first1=Paul |title=NRL dismissal rule must keep pace with the evolution of the game |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-dismissal-rule-must-keep-pace-with-the-evolution-of-the-game/news-story/42097052ee59eec83201dd0b9a3c2239 |website=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=7 April 2019}}</ref> [[Illawarra Steelers]] and [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] before moving to England to play in the [[Super League]] for the Leeds Rhinos.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds' double signing |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/leeds-double-signing-1316584.html |work=The Independent |access-date=7 April 2019}}</ref> He was later awarded Leeds Rhinos heritage player number [[List_of_Leeds_Rhinos_players|1291]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Daly |first1=Phil |title=Ever popular prop Masella catches up with Gary in Australia |url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2015/12/04/ever-popular-prop-masella-catches-up-with-gary-in-australia/ |website=Leeds Rhinos |access-date=7 April 2019}}</ref> After leaving the Rhinos, Masella played for Wakefield Trinity Wildcats and [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Whalley |first1=John |title=Wakefield are saved after creditors reach agreement |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/4773043/Wakefield-are-saved-after-creditors-reach-agreement.html |website=The Daily Telegraph |date=5 October 2000 |access-date=7 April 2019}}</ref>

In September 2000, at the height of a financial crisis at the [[Wakefield_Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], the contracts of all players aged over 24 were terminated. The players affected were; [[Andy Fisher (rugby league)|Andy Fisher]], [[Bobbie Goulding]], [[Warren Jowitt]], [[Tony Kemp (rugby league)|Tony Kemp]] (player-coach), [[Steve McNamara]], [[Francis Maloney (rugby league)|Francis Maloney]], Martin Masella, [[Steve Prescott]], [[Bright Sodje]], [[Francis Stephenson]] and [[Glen Tomlinson]]. Masella was one of a group of players who were awarded compensation from an industrial tribunal as a result of the dismissal.<ref name="Goodway calls for aid package">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1295270.stm|title=Goodway calls for aid package|publisher=BBC|date=2001-04-24|access-date=2010-01-01}}</ref>

Masella came out of retirement to play for Tonga against [[Cumbria_rugby_league_team|Cumbria]] at the age of 36.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ex-Rhino Masella takes on the Cumbrians at 36 |url=https://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/16798017.ex-rhino-masella-takes-on-the-cumbrians-at-36/ |website=News and Star with the Cumberland News |date=25 October 2006 |access-date=7 April 2019}}</ref>
